2606.08000.title: Summarization is Not Dead Yet
2606.08000.paper_summary: The progress of large language models (LLMs) has fueled claims that model-generated summaries rival or even surpass human-written references, raising questions about whether summarization remains an open research problem. We re-examine this narrative through a multi-track evaluation covering five diverse datasets and five state-of-the-art LLMs, combining controlled human assessment, bias-mitigated LLM-as-Judge protocols, factuality verification against external knowledge, and corpus-level linguistic analysis. Our findings reveal a more nuanced landscape in which human reference summaries continue to demonstrate advantages in informativeness and faithfulness, whereas LLM outputs are preferred mainly for surface-level coherence and fluency. Factuality verification indicates that human references remain more reliable, particularly for claims involving reasoning or synthesis, and linguistic analysis uncovers a pattern of stylistic homogeneity across different models. These observations suggest that current LLMs have raised the floor of summarization quality, but the ceiling of their performance remains below human capabilities.
2606.08000.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）的進展引發了模型生成的摘要與人類撰寫的參考資料相媲美甚至超越的說法，這引發了關於摘要是否仍然是一個開放研究問題的疑問。<lb/>我們通過一項多軌評估重新檢視這一敘事，涵蓋五個多樣化的數據集和五個最先進的LLMs，結合了受控的人類評估、減少偏見的LLM作為評判者的協議、對外部知識的事實驗證，以及語料庫層面的語言分析。<lb/>我們的研究結果揭示了一個更為細緻的格局，在這個格局中，人類參考摘要在信息量和忠實度方面仍然顯示出優勢，而LLM的輸出主要在表面的一致性和流暢性上受到青睞。<lb/>事實驗證顯示，人類參考資料在可靠性方面仍然更高，特別是對於涉及推理或綜合的主張，而語言分析則揭示了不同模型之間的風格同質性模式。<lb/>這些觀察表明，當前的LLMs提高了摘要質量的底線，但它們的性能上限仍低於人類的能力。
2606.08000.author: Dongqi Liu et.al.
2606.08000.authors: Dongqi Liu, Chenxi Whitehouse, Zheng Zhao, Zhuchen Cao, Jian Li, Yabiao Wang
